title: What is quantum-safe cryptography?
summary: NCSC-NL explainer stating that the most effective way to mitigate the quantum-computer threat is to migrate to quantum-safe cryptography (QVC), also called post-quantum cryptography.

title: NIST releases three quantum-safe cryptography standards: what does this mean for your organisation?
summary: NCSC-NL expert blog on NIST's publication of three post-quantum cryptography standards on 13 August 2024 and what the new quantum-safe standards mean for Dutch organisations.

title: Make your organization quantum secure: kicking off your quantum migration program
summary: NCSC-NL guidance document (English edition) on starting a quantum migration programme, defining post-quantum cryptography (PQC) as cryptography impervious to the threat of quantum computing.

title: TNO: Cryptographic Asset Discovery and Inventory
summary: NCSC-NL page presenting a TNO approach to cryptographic asset discovery and inventory as a key first step for organisations that must migrate to quantum-safe cryptography in time.

title: Speech by State Secretary Van Marum at the Post-Quantum Cryptography symposium
summary: Speech delivered by State Secretary Eddie van Marum at the Post Quantum Cryptography symposium on 2 December 2025, published by the Dutch central government.

title: The PQC Migration Handbook (revised and extended second edition)
summary: Joint AIVD, CWI and TNO handbook providing guidelines for migrating to post-quantum cryptography, supporting organisations in mitigating the quantum-computer threat to cryptography; revised and extended second edition.

title: Letter to Parliament accompanying the renewed handbook for quantum-safe cryptography
summary: Letter in which Minister Uitermark (Interior and Kingdom Relations) informs the House of Representatives about the renewed handbook for the migration to quantum-safe cryptography.

title: Decision memorandum accompanying the letter to Parliament on the renewed quantum-safe cryptography handbook
summary: Decision memorandum containing background information on the letter to Parliament about the renewed handbook for quantum-safe cryptography.

title: Quantum computers are coming and the Netherlands is prepared
summary: Government news item on the central-government programme Quantumveilige Cryptografie Rijk, which develops tools to mitigate the risks quantum technology poses to current security systems.

title: Protect yourself now against the threat of quantum computers (2026)
summary: AIVD publication urging organisations to protect themselves against the quantum-computer threat, referencing the central-government programme Quantumveilige Cryptografie Nederland (QvC NL) set up to support the central government and other organisations.

title: Securing Tomorrow, Today: Transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography
summary: Statement on the transition to post-quantum cryptography hosted by the AIVD, warning of the risk that the migration to quantum-safe cryptography will not be completed in time.

title: Prepare for the threat of quantum computers
summary: AIVD brochure advising organisations to prepare now for migration to quantum-safe cryptography and explaining how to secure systems against the threat of quantum computers.

title: Answers to parliamentary questions from MP Sneller on quantum-proof encryption
summary: Government answers to parliamentary questions on quantum-proof encryption, addressing quantum-safe cryptography, the opportunities it offers, and risks such as insufficient awareness and knowledge.

title: Decision memorandum on the report concerning new EU Commission proposals (PQC and QKD)
summary: Decision memorandum from the Ministry of the Interior and Kingdom Relations, published via the House of Representatives, distinguishing post-quantum cryptography (based on mathematical problems that cannot be broken by quantum computers) from quantum key distribution in the context of EU proposals.

title: Communication on the EU Quantum Strategy (parliamentary document)
summary: Parliamentary document on the EU quantum strategy referencing the Dutch programme Quantumveilige Cryptografie NL, which supports vital sectors in migrating to post-quantum encryption and contributes to the European effort.

title: New tools available to mitigate the threat of quantum computers
summary: News item on the Dutch digital government portal announcing the updated handbook on quantum-safe cryptography and the PQChoiceAssistant tool, which support governments in future-proofing their security.

title: Security guidelines for Transport Layer Security (TLS) version 2025-05
summary: NCSC-NL TLS security guidelines (version 2025-05, published 26 June 2025) that explicitly address hybrid quantum-safe key-exchange algorithms (X25519MLKEM768, SecP256r1MLKEM768, SecP384r1MLKEM1024 combining ECDHE with ML-KEM) and advise organisations, by quantum-risk profile, on when to implement quantum-safe cryptography in TLS.

title: Quantum computers are coming, and the Netherlands is ready
summary: English-language Government.nl news item (17 July 2025) announcing that the Dutch central government has an operational quantum network and is deploying post-quantum cryptography alongside quantum key distribution to protect sensitive data, as demonstrated at a pre-NATO Summit event. (English mirror of the Dutch rijksoverheid.nl item at a distinct URL.)

title: Fiche: Simplificatie NIS2-richtlijn (parliamentary letter, new EU Commission proposals)
issuingOrg: Tweede Kamer / Ministerie van Buitenlandse Zaken
summary: Dutch government assessment (BNC-fiche, 6 March 2026) of the European Commission proposal to simplify the NIS2 Directive, which explicitly notes the Commission's proposal that member states make post-quantum cryptography a mandatory part of the national cybersecurity strategy; the PQC reference is incidental to a NIS2-focused fiche, hence flagged for review.
